stories.title: Eala Stuns Defending Champion Swiatek 7-6(9), 6-2 — First Filipina in Grand Slam Second Week

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Alexandra Eala makes Wimbledon history — def. defending champ Swiatek 7-6(9), 6-2. She's the first Filipino player, man or woman, to reach a Grand Slam second week in the Open Era. "Being here is a blessing." #Wimbledon #Eala #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Eala broke Swiatek 5 times, won 68% on her second serve, survived an 11-9 tiebreak, then won the second set 6-2. Final: 7-6(9), 6-2. History. Next: No. 13 Paolini in the Round of 16. #Wimbledon #Eala #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

article.headline: Eala Stuns Swiatek 7-6(9), 6-2 to Become First Filipina in Grand Slam Second Week

stories.title: Mertens (No. 25) Upsets No. 2 Rybakina 7-6(4), 6-1 — Day 6 Double Shock

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Elise Mertens (No. 25) def. No. 2 Rybakina 7-6(4), 6-1 in 96 minutes. First top-10 grass win of Mertens' career. Combined with Eala def. Swiatek: both No. 2 and No. 3 seeds gone on the same afternoon. #Wimbledon #WTA #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Day 6 at Wimbledon: No. 2 Rybakina out. No. 3 Swiatek out. Mertens faces Bouzkova in R4. The women's draw is wide open. #Wimbledon #WTA #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

article.headline: Mertens Shocks No. 2 Rybakina 7-6(4), 6-1 in 96 Minutes — First Grass Top-10 Win of Her Career

stories.title: Bublik def. Tiafoe in Marathon Five-Setter; Zverev, Keys Advance — Day 6 Round-Up

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Bublik hit 48 aces and saved 9 set points in a 13-11 tiebreak to beat No. 17 Tiafoe 4-6, 7-6(5), 7-6(13-11), 4-6, 6-3 in 4+ hours. Also: Zverev def. Giron 6-2, 7-6, 6-4. Keys def. Anisimova 3-6, 6-2, 6-3. #Wimbledon #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Wimbledon Day 7 Preview — Djokovic Chases Sole Record, Sabalenka vs Osaka Headlines

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text: Novak Djokovic has 105 Wimbledon wins — tied with Roger Federer for the all-time men's record. Win No. 106 today vs qualifier Safiullin gives him sole possession. Centre Court, 1:30 PM local. #Wimbledon #Djokovic #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text: Day 7 at Wimbledon has everything: Djokovic chasing the all-time wins record vs Safiullin. Sabalenka vs Osaka — first-ever grass meeting. Defending champ Sinner vs qualifier Mochizuki. #Wimbledon #Tennis #WTA #TennisFanrecap

article.headline: Day 7 Preview: Djokovic Chases Sole Wimbledon Record, Sabalenka vs Osaka Headlines Centre Court

stories.title: Open Women's Draw — Swiatek and Rybakina Out, Who Claims Wimbledon?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Wimbledon women's draw after Day 6 — Defending champ Swiatek: OUT. No. 2 Rybakina: OUT. No. 1 Sabalenka still in (vs Osaka, Day 7). No. 31 Eala still in (vs Paolini, Day 8). The draw is wide open. #Wimbledon #WTA #Tennis #TennisFanrecap
